clinical_criteria: Documented behaviors from WAC 388-106-0100 list: assaultive behavior, combative during personal care, irritability/agitation not easily altered, hallucinations, delusions, yelling/screaming 4+ days/week, resistive behavior
Behavior Point Score (BPS) >4 contributes to Group B; BPS >12 supports B-High
Confirmed irreversible dementia diagnosis (Alzheimer's, vascular, Lewy body, frontotemporal) supports EARC-SDC eligibility per WAC 388-106-0033
Behaviors require staff intervention beyond redirection (1:1 supervision, behavioral care plan)
wac_citation: WAC 388-106-0100 (mood/behavior and behavior point score); WAC 388-106-0125 (Group B classification); WAC 388-106-0033 (specialized dementia care eligibility); DSHS Specialized Behavior Support (SBS) specialty contract
disclosure_language_required: Per RCW 70.128.280 and DSHS Form 10-409, AFH must disclose whether it accepts residents with dementia, the specific behavioral symptoms it can safely manage (e.g., agitation, resistive care, verbal aggression), and whether the home holds a Specialized Behavior Support contract. If unable to manage a behavior, the AFH must state so explicitly to support move-out or non-admission decisions.

clinical_criteria: Cognitive Performance Score (CPS) 3-6 indicating moderate to severe impairment
Documented impairment in short-term memory, decision-making, daily-task comprehension, or making self understood
CPS 4-6 with clinical complexity drives Group D; CPS <4 with clinical complexity drives Group C
Requires structured cues, supervision during ADLs, or 24-hour orientation support
wac_citation: WAC 388-106-0090 (cognitive performance score); WAC 388-106-0125 (Group C/D classification)
disclosure_language_required: Per RCW 70.128.280, AFH must disclose its capacity to provide cognitive supervision, structured routines, and dementia-friendly environment. If the home advertises 'specialty dementia care,' it must comply with WAC 388-76 special care designation requirements, including additional caregiver training in cognitive support and disorientation management.

clinical_criteria: Depression score >=14 on standardized scale (PHQ-9 or CARE depression indicator)
Crying/tearfulness 4+ days/week (per WAC 388-106-0100 indicator list)
Documented anxious complaints, repetitive verbal complaints, or expressions of unrealistic fears
Requires coordinated mental health follow-up or psychotropic medication oversight
wac_citation: WAC 388-106-0100 (mood/behavior; depression score is an enumerated indicator); WAC 388-106-0125 (Group B classification when combined with ADL or BPS thresholds)
disclosure_language_required: Per RCW 70.128.280, AFH must disclose its capacity to support residents with diagnosed mood disorders, including coordination with outpatient mental health providers and PRN psychotropic medication administration. If the resident's mood symptoms exceed the home's behavioral support capability, the AFH must clearly state non-acceptance.

clinical_criteria: 7 or more prescribed medications, or multiple daily administration times
Sliding scale or PRN protocols requiring caregiver judgment
Medication requires nurse delegation per RCW 18.79.260 / WAC 388-76-10405 (e.g., injections, eye drops by unlicensed staff)
Time-sensitive medications (Parkinson's, anti-seizure, anticoagulants)
wac_citation: WAC 388-106-0095 (clinical complexity includes injections); WAC 388-76-10405 (when AFH cannot provide nursing care, must contract with licensed nurse); RCW 18.79.260 (nurse delegation)
disclosure_language_required: Per DSHS Form 10-409, AFH must disclose its level of medication service: (a) administration of oral/topical/eye/ear medications, (b) administration via nurse delegation, or (c) assistance only with resident self-administration. AFH must disclose whether it manages refills, secure storage, and high-risk medications (anticoagulants, opioids, insulin).

clinical_criteria: Insulin-dependent diabetes mellitus (type 1 or insulin-requiring type 2)
Blood glucose monitoring 2-4 times daily
Sliding-scale insulin or carbohydrate-counting regimen
History of hypoglycemic events requiring intervention
wac_citation: WAC 388-106-0095 (Diabetes Mellitus insulin-dependent and injections are clinical complexity factors); WAC 388-76-10405 (skilled nursing scope); RCW 18.79.260 (nurse delegation required for insulin administration by unlicensed staff)
disclosure_language_required: Per RCW 70.128.280 and DSHS Form 10-409 ('Clinical Services' section: 'Blood glucose monitoring'), AFH must disclose whether it performs blood glucose monitoring and whether it administers insulin (and under what authority: delegating RN, contracted nurse, or family/resident self-administration). If insulin administration is beyond scope, the AFH must state so explicitly.

clinical_criteria: Total dependence (ADL score 4) for transfers per WAC 388-106-0105
Requires two-staff assist or mechanical lift (Hoyer, sit-to-stand) for bed-to-chair transfers
Cumulative ADL score >=22 supports Group E (exceptional care) classification when combined with turning/repositioning
Documented inability to bear weight or follow transfer cues
wac_citation: WAC 388-106-0105 (ADL scoring, transfers); WAC 388-106-0110 (exceptional care, ADL >=22); WAC 388-106-0125 (Group E)
disclosure_language_required: Per DSHS Form 10-409 ('Mobility/Transfers' section), AFH must disclose its capacity for 'one-on-one transfer assistance, use of lifts, belts, board as needed.' If the home cannot safely staff a two-person transfer (e.g., single caregiver overnight), it must disclose this limitation; admission of a two-person-transfer resident under those conditions risks WAC 388-76 enforcement action.

clinical_criteria: Exit-seeking wander (WAC 388-106-0100 behavior category 23-28, behavior value 4-5)
History of elopement or attempted elopement
Non-exit-seeking wander (category 1-8, behavior value 0.5-1) when frequent
Requires perimeter alarms, locked egress, or 1:1 line-of-sight supervision
wac_citation: WAC 388-106-0100 (wandering enumerated; exit-seeking carries highest behavior value); WAC 388-106-0125 (Group B classification driver); DSHS Specialized Behavior Support (SBS) contract may apply
disclosure_language_required: Per RCW 70.128.280, AFH must disclose whether the physical plant has secured/alarmed egress and whether staff coverage supports a wanderer (especially overnight). If the home holds itself out as accepting dementia/wandering residents, it must comply with WAC 388-76 special-care designation requirements. If the home cannot safely manage exit-seeking, it must state so to prevent inappropriate admission.

clinical_criteria: Pressure injury (any stage) per WAC 388-106-0095 clinical complexity criteria
Open lesions, stasis ulcers, or burns requiring dressing/wound care
Stage 3-4 pressure injury or unstageable wound requiring skilled-nurse-led wound protocol
Turning/repositioning program required (contributes to Group E per WAC 388-106-0110)
wac_citation: WAC 388-106-0095 (pressure injuries, open lesions, stasis ulcers, burns are clinical complexity); WAC 388-106-0110 (turning/repositioning + ADL >=22 = exceptional care); WAC 388-76-10405 (AFH must contract licensed nurse for wound care beyond its scope)
disclosure_language_required: Per WAC 388-76-10405, if the resident requires nursing-level wound care, the AFH must either employ or contract with a licensed nurse to provide that care or arrange nurse delegation. The disclosure must state whether the home provides Stage 1-2 wound care only, or contracts for higher-acuity wound management.

clinical_criteria: Frequent or total bladder/bowel incontinence per WAC 388-106-0095
Scheduled toileting plan or bowel program in place
Indwelling, intermittent, or external catheter
Ostomy care required
wac_citation: WAC 388-106-0095 (frequent/total incontinence, scheduled toileting, bowel programs, ostomy care, and catheter management are clinical complexity factors)
disclosure_language_required: Per RCW 70.128.280, AFH must disclose its capacity to manage bowel and bladder incontinence, including frequency of brief changes, catheter care, ostomy care, and scheduled toileting. Ostomy and catheter care may require nurse delegation per WAC 388-76-10405.

clinical_criteria: Extensive assistance (ADL score 3) or total dependence (ADL score 4) for toilet use per WAC 388-106-0105
Requires staff assist for transfer-to-toilet, brief changes, peri-care
Documented falls or near-falls during toileting attempts
disclosure_language_required: Per DSHS Form 10-409 ('Personal Hygiene' section: 'Assistance provided, one on one guide, pericare'), AFH must disclose level of toileting assistance (independent prompting, hands-on assist, total dependence). Disclosure must align with the negotiated care plan.

clinical_criteria: Extensive assistance or total dependence for eating (ADL score 3-4) per WAC 388-106-0105
Swallowing problems with eating dependence per WAC 388-106-0095 (clinical complexity)
Modified-texture diet (puree, mechanical soft) or thickened liquids
Tube feeding providing >50% of calories supports Group E per WAC 388-106-0110
wac_citation: WAC 388-106-0105 (ADL scoring: eating); WAC 388-106-0095 (swallowing problems with eating dependence is clinical complexity); WAC 388-106-0110 (tube feeding >50% calories supports exceptional care)
disclosure_language_required: Per DSHS Form 10-409 ('Nutrition/Feeding' section: 'Spoon feeding, blended diets, sugar free, fiber accommodations'), AFH must disclose feeding levels it supports: independent, set-up only, hands-on assist, spoon-feeding, and texture-modified diets. Tube feeding capability must be explicitly disclosed and requires nurse delegation under WAC 388-76-10405.

code_name: Fall risk / fall history requiring intervention
clinical_criteria: Two or more falls in the prior 30 days, or any fall with injury
Pathological bone fracture history per WAC 388-106-0095 (clinical complexity)
Gait instability requiring assistive device (walker, cane, wheelchair) and supervised ambulation
ADL locomotion score >=2 (limited assistance or greater) per WAC 388-106-0105
Cognitive impairment combined with mobility deficit (compound fall risk)
wac_citation: WAC 388-106-0105 (ADL locomotion scoring); WAC 388-106-0095 (pathological bone fracture is clinical complexity)
disclosure_language_required: Per RCW 70.128.280 and DSHS Form 10-409 ('Mobility/Transfers' section: 'walker, wheelchair, one on one guide, assistance, accommodations are made'), AFH must disclose fall-prevention measures (bed alarms, low beds, perimeter monitoring), supervised ambulation availability, and whether 1:1 staffing during high-risk transfers is feasible given staffing ratios.
